name: Wet Letters
classification_notes: A typeface built entirely from photographs of physical wet letter forms, placing it firmly in the experimental/display category with no conventional typographic construction method.
summary: A photographic typeface built from Shannon Ebner's wet letter alphabet images, released as both a usable font and an artist edition in collaboration with Source Type.
description: Each glyph is drawn directly from Ebner's photographs of wet letters, giving the typeface a material, indexical quality absent from virtually all digital type. The project originates from the broader STRAY WORLD collaboration with David Reinfurt and has appeared across exhibitions at kaufmann repetto and Altman Siegel between 2019 and 2025. Available in OTF and TTF formats, it sits at the boundary between fine art publication and functional typeface.
foundry_blurb: Source Type describes Wet Letters as a photographic typeface and artist edition produced in collaboration with artist Shannon Ebner, whose wet letter alphabet images have featured in exhibitions including THE SEAWEED SYNTHESIZER and WET WORDS IN A HOT FIELD. The font is part of the STRAY WORLD project by Ebner and David Reinfurt, published by Source Type in 2022, and is available to purchase as a font or artist edition via the Source Type Store.
